title: Oxytropis jarovoji
taxonomy: Oxytropis jarovoji is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Oxytropis, which includes several species of subalpine and alpine plants. Oxytropis jarovoji is native to Central Asia, specifically in the mountainous regions of Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, and Tajikistan. The plant is typically found growing in rocky and grassy areas at high elevations. As a member of the Fabaceae family, it is also known as a legume due to its ability to form symbiotic relationships with nitrogen-fixing bacteria.
